mnemonics: Bayes' Ball Bounces Both Ways (to remember Bayes' Theorem considers evidence in both directions, P(A|B) and P(B|A))
keyPoints: Bayes' Theorem updates the probability of a hypothesis as more evidence becomes available.
Probabilistic reasoning handles uncertainty by assigning degrees of belief.
Statistical models use data to make inferences and predictions rather than relying on strict logical rules.
commonMistakes: Confusing prior probability P(H) with posterior probability P(H|E).
Assuming all events are independent when they are not, leading to incorrect probability calculations.
